sources: Avoma call 2025-01-28 (Introductory call: Zycus X SaskTel)
metrics.narrative: SaskTel's stated Business_Objectives__c call for 'rapid and mass user adoption with a shorter time to ROI realization' and 'accelerated achievement of sourcing and contracting goals,' and Value_to_Customer__c cites 'cost savings and process efficiency with seamless ERP integration.' These are qualification-era CRM narrative fields rather than a quantified figure voiced by a named buyer on a call, since the only Avoma-captured session (introductory call, 28 Jan 2025) was purely social with no business content discussed. No dollar figure, percentage, or specific KPI has ever been confirmed directly by SaskTel.

champion.narrative: X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c is empty and no contact has shown advocacy behavior. Jeffrey Buzila has been a reliable, responsive point of contact for RFP-process logistics (deadlines, status updates), most recently replying 13 Apr 2026, but every message is a process update, not internal selling or privileged insight into scoring. Adriel Picard, the named Decision Maker, has not replied to either of the two most recent Horizon event invitations (sent 4 May 2026 and 4 Jun 2026, resent 8 Jul 2026). No one on the account is currently behaving as a champion.

competition.narrative: Competitors__c names four rivals in this RFP: Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba, with SAP Ariba also the incumbent SaskTel is actively trying to replace ('Procurement Tool: Ariba (under evaluation for replacement)'). A further vendor was named in an early qualification-era note (Why_not_Zycus__c) but does not reappear in the current Competitors__c field, so it is carried as an unnamed fifth vendor, unconfirmed for the live 2026 RFP. No scorecard result or buyer-voiced preference among the finalists has been shared with Zycus.

identify_pain.narrative: Customer_Business_Problem__c documents specific dissatisfaction with the incumbent, SAP Ariba: 'high cost, low value,' 'manual invoicing... leading to inefficiencies,' 'poor supplier experience,' and a 'fragmented procurement setup.' This is a detailed, credible pain narrative, but it lives in the CRM qualification record rather than being voiced by SaskTel on a recorded call; the single Avoma-captured call (28 Jan 2025) never touched on business pain.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting artifact (MSA, Order Form, SOW) has been raised in any source, and Current_Contract_Expiration__c is blank. The deal has not progressed past the RFP/shortlisting stage into commercial or legal discussion; no pricing conversation has occurred with SaskTel in any call or email on record.

economic_buyer.narrative: Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c is false and no economic-buyer or budget-approval field (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c) is populated. The two people on the opportunity are Adriel Picard, Director of Procurement (Decision Maker contact role), and Jeffrey Buzila, who is coordinating the RFP process; neither carries confirmed commercial or budget authority for a $357,898 multi-year S2P investment. As of 8 Jul 2026, the deal owner planned to reach out to a CTO and a EVP on the account to learn whether either sits on the steering committee making the decision, but that outreach had not yet occurred as of this sweep (9 Jul 2026).

decision_process.narrative: The process mechanics are unusually well documented in Next_Step__c: RFP issued 11 Feb 2026, Q&A window closing 25 Feb 2026, submission deadline 11 Mar 2026 (met, per the '3.11.26 KK — RFP submitted' entry), a shortlisting announcement the week of 6 Apr 2026 confirming Zycus advanced, and supplier presentations/interviews originally planned for the week of 20 Apr 2026. That presentation round has not occurred as of 9 Jul 2026, roughly 11 weeks (~80 days) past its planned window, and no new date has been confirmed.

decision_criteria.narrative: Business_Requirements__c and Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c are both empty/false, so no published evaluation rubric is on file with the relevant stakeholder own timeline confirms a structured formal evaluation is underway (issue date 11 Feb 2026, questions due 18 Feb 2026, SaskTel answers due 25 Feb 2026, submission deadline 11 Mar 2026 2:00pm CST), but the actual weighting or scoring criteria used to compare vendors has not been shared with Zycus.

items.name: Close date pushed ~4 months
items.summary: Close date moved from 2026-04-30 to 2026-09-04, a slip of over four months with no accompanying activity logged — suggests the deal stalled or was deprioritized rather than progressing toward the original date.

day_summary.overall: The only signal today is a big close-date slip on SaskTel, pushed from end of April all the way to September — over four months out. No emails or calls logged alongside it, so this reads as a quiet re-forecasting rather than a specific event; the deal has effectively been parked, and someone should find out what's actually stalled before treating the new date as real.

cro_panel.intro: Structurally an RFP finalist (Shortlisted since 25 Nov 2025) that delivered its RFP response on time, but 226 days into the Shortlisted stage against an ~18-day playbook cadence, with no economic buyer engaged, no champion, no commercial conversation, and the mandatory supplier-presentation round now roughly 11 weeks overdue with no new date.; Momentum is rep-driven, not buyer-driven; the account owner is actively chasing (last outreach 8 Jul 2026), but SaskTel's last inbound reply was 87 days ago (13 Apr 2026)

reasons.detail: SaskTel ($357,898, forecast Best Case, stage Shortlisted) has logged zero activity for 79 days. Mike Flowers' 2026-07-02 next-step note confirms Jeff Buzila says the team is still tallying RFP scores, and SaskTel has not responded to either the Horizon (Sept) or Calgary Stampede invites we sent. With no champion identified and no visibility into Zycus's rank in the four-vendor S2P RFP (Coupa, GEP, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba), a deal carried in Best Case risks a silent loss at down-selection and an inflated forecast.

north_star_verdict.math: Today 8 Jul 2026: 58 days to close (4 Sep 2026). Deal has been in Shortlisted stage since 24 Nov 2025 (~226 days / ~7.5 months dwell). Supplier presentations planned for the week of 20 Apr 2026 never occurred (~79 days / ~11 weeks overdue as of today). Last genuine two-way buyer reply was Jeffrey Buzila's email on 13 Apr 2026 (86 days ago); two Horizon invites (5 May, 4 Jun 2026) plus a third resent today (8 Jul 2026) are unanswered. No demo has ever been conducted (SF Gaps_identified_during_sales_demo__c: 'Demo yet to be conducted'). Remaining motion (presentation -> commercial -> EB sign-off -> contracting) realistically needs 90-120+ days, exceeding the 58 days left by 30-60+ days.
evidence: Presentation round planned for week of 20 Apr 2026 never occurred; ~11 weeks overdue as of 8 Jul 2026 (SF Next_Step_History__c).
Last confirmed two-way buyer reply: Jeffrey Buzila, 13 Apr 2026 (86 days ago); three subsequent Horizon invites unanswered (SF Next_Step_History__c / Task, 8 Jul 2026).

north_star_verdict.headline: Presentation gate ~11 weeks overdue and buyer silent for 86 days; RFP scoring stalled with 58 days left to a 4 Sep 2026 close and no economic buyer or demo after 18 months.

flags: Multi-thread beyond Adriel Picard (Director of Procurement) and Jeffrey Buzila to map and engage the economic buyer (likely one of the CFOs: the account shows Charlene Gavel, Chief Financial Officer, or Mike Anderson, CFO, or a VP Operations) — a $358k S2P transformation will require C-level sign-off and no CFO or exec is on the opportunity contact roles or has appeared on a call.

Activate Merlin and Agentic AI differentiation during the presentation round — the RFP explicitly includes Merlin and Agentic AI in scope, giving Zycus a wedge vs Coupa/GEP/JAGGAER/Ariba's traditional SaaS; prove AI-led sourcing and negotiation ROI with a use-case demo and quantify the business case to stand out in the scorecard tallying.

In a 4-vendor RFP already scoring responses, get Merlin/Agentic AI in front of the evaluation committee before or during the presentation round, since no differentiation has been demonstrated yet (SF Next_Step__c 6.4.26MF).

Convert Jeffrey Buzila and Adriel Picard from passive RFP responders into an active champion by giving them an internal-facing business case to carry forward, since neither has advocated or driven the agenda to date.

59 days to 4 Sep close; even if presentation occurred tomorrow, commercial, EB approval, legal, and contracting would require 90-120 days; close date is unrealistic

best_practice_check.summary: The highest-leverage move is broadening access beyond the two current process-level contacts; the process itself is stalled at the presentation-round gate.

customer_preference.evidence: No call or email captures SaskTel voicing a preference for Zycus over Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, or SAP Ariba; the RFP scorecard result has not been shared (Next_Step__c dated log, through 2026-07-08).

competitive_position.summary: SaskTel is running a formal four-vendor RFP (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba) with SAP Ariba as the incumbent being displaced; all four currently carry an undifferentiated medium threat level because no buyer preference has surfaced since the 11 Mar 2026 submission. SAP Ariba is the single strongest current threat by virtue of being the entrenched status quo ('do nothing / stay on Ariba' risk), not because of any stated buyer preference for it. A fifth name (mentioned only in an early qualification-era risk note) never appeared in the formal RFP field and is treated as faded/dormant.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Structurally an RFP finalist (Shortlisted since 25 Nov 2025) that delivered its RFP response on time, but 226 days into the Shortlisted stage against an ~18-day playbook cadence, with no economic buyer engaged, no champion, no commercial conversation, and the mandatory supplier-presentation round now roughly 11 weeks overdue with no new date. Momentum is rep-driven, not buyer-driven: the account owner is actively chasing (last outreach 8 Jul 2026), but SaskTel's last inbound reply was 87 days ago (13 Apr 2026).

deal_risk.text: Close date has already slipped three times (originally 31 Dec 2025 -> 28 Feb 2026 -> 30 Apr 2026 -> 4 Sep 2026); with 57 days left and the presentation round still not rescheduled, commercials, an EB proposal, and contracting cannot realistically complete by 4 Sep 2026.

Forecast is Best Case with no supporting evidence — no economic buyer, no champion, no pricing conversation, and an 87-day buyer silence streak underlie a category that implies high confidence of closing this quarter.

win_position.text: Economic buyer gate unmet — Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c is false and no C-level SaskTel contact has ever engaged on this deal; the only two contacts (Adriel Picard, Jeffrey Buzila) carry no confirmed budget authority for the $357,898 deal.

Anchor badly eroded by dwell — the opportunity has sat in Shortlisted for 226 days (since 25 Nov 2025) against an ~18-day expected cadence for this stage, with the mandatory presentation round (week of 20 Apr 2026) never held.

RFP execution has been clean — Zycus met every stated deadline (open questions 18 Feb 2026, RFP response 11 Mar 2026 2:00pm CST) and was confirmed shortlisted the week of 6 Apr 2026 among four named finalists.

No champion or competitive edge evidenced — Jeffrey Buzila's replies are process-status only, Adriel Picard (the named Decision Maker) has not answered two consecutive Horizon invites (4 May, 4 Jun 2026), and there is no signal on how Zycus scores against Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, or the incumbent SAP Ariba.

deal_momentum.text: Buyer-side silence since 13 Apr 2026 — the last confirmed inbound reply from SaskTel is now 87 days old, despite the deal owner sending renewed outreach on 4 Jun and 8 Jul 2026.

Planned RFP milestone missed — the supplier-presentation/interview round scheduled for the week of 20 Apr 2026 (~80 days ago) never occurred and has no confirmed reschedule, so process-mode protection for a 'quiet-but-on-track' RFP no longer applies.

The account owner is actively working the deal — a follow-up email to Adriel Picard went out 8 Jul 2026 (1 day before this sweep), with a concrete next escalation planned (reaching the CTO and CIO/EVP to map the steering committee).
